title: THE SURVIVOR — LIFELINE CUT V5
episodeObjective: Lock channel 7.14, establish believable real-time contact with Maya Chen, guide her through the lower access spine, manage the Hale/Maya conflict, and end by deciding whether to press Maya's memory or keep her moving.
directionRules: Every episode has a concrete objective.
Characters stay physically active and move through real spaces.
Every decision has a visible consequence.
Matching puzzles are minimized or removed.
System messages are minimized and used only for terminal-critical feedback.
Trust is never displayed directly; it is expressed through dialogue.
The player always receives exactly two choices when choices appear.
Characters can react negatively to each other and to the player.
Every character has a distinct voice.
The story feels like a live rescue operation, not a static chat.

frequency_714.title: SIGNAL TUNING
frequency_714.description: Tune the emergency frequency until Maya's unstable survivor transmission locks.

e04_n01.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: [OPENING CHANNEL 7.14]

e04_n01.objective: Open channel 7.14 and catch the first unstable survivor transmission.

e04_n02_first_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: ...hello?
No. Wait.
Someone answered.
Please say something back.
Not a code. Not a protocol. Just something a person would say.
My relay is drifting and I can't hold this channel for long.

e04_n02_first_voice.objective: The player chooses whether to connect emotionally first or treat Maya as an unknown signal.

choices.text: TELL HER YOU CAN HEAR HER.

e04_n03_identify.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Okay.
Okay, that's a voice. That's a real answer.
I forgot how much that matters.
I can't give you everything yet. The signal keeps tearing at the edges.
Keep me on the channel and I'll tell you who I am.

e04_n03_identify.objective: Maya reacts better to direct human contact but still needs technical help.

e04_n03_verify.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: You sound like mission control.
Annoying.
Useful, maybe.
Fine. Verify me. I would verify me too.
I've been alone long enough to stop trusting anything that answers too quickly.

e04_n03_verify.objective: Maya is irritated by verification, but respects the discipline.

e04_n03_trace.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.title: EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT
events.content:
CHANNEL: 7.14 MHz
SIGNAL STATE: DISTORTED
VOICEPRINT: UNKNOWN FEMALE
RECOVERED FRAGMENTS:

"...anyone..."
"...relay is drifting..."
"...not Hale..."
"...lower access spine..."

Recommended action: manual signal tuning required.
events.caption: Text reconstruction of distorted emergency audio from channel 7.14.

events.text: That's my channel.
Or what's left of it.
The carrier keeps sliding under me. It feels like trying to stand on a floor that forgot it was solid.
If you can tune it, do it now.
If you wait, I go back to shouting into static.

e04_n03_trace.objective: Player decides whether to trust the new signal or consult Hale first.

e04_n04_hale_warning.speaker: HALE

events.text: Do not let that channel lead.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Tune it if you have to. But keep control of the carrier.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If she gives you a name, don't assume the name makes her safe.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Names are easy. Signals lie better than people.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And yes, I know that includes me.
events.speaker: HALE

e04_n04_hale_warning.objective: Hale warns the operator without fully stopping the rescue.

e04_n04_tune.speaker: SYSTEM
e04_n04_tune.objective: Tune 7.14 without letting the carrier drift out of reach.

e04_n04_fail.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: [SIGNAL LOCK FAILED]
No— don't let it drift.
I had you for a second. Don't make me go back to static.
Hold it steady when it bites. The drift gets worse right before the lock.
[MANUAL RETUNE REQUIRED]

e04_n04_fail.objective: Failure no longer stalls; the player can retry or review a clue.

e04_n04_hint.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Listen. Not to the words. To the pull.
It drifts below seven, then snaps back. It wants you to overcorrect.
Don't chase it. Let it come to 7.14 and hold there.
I hate that I sound like Hale right now.
Just... lock the signal.

e04_n04_hint.objective: Offer a clue while preserving the two-choice rule.

choices.text: ASK HALE FOR A STABILIZATION TIP.

e04_n04_hale_tip.speaker: HALE

events.text: Fine. Technical answer.
Don't chase the carrier. Predict the drift. If the frequency is fighting you, it is probably trying to make you move too much.
Small corrections. Hold on the target. Let the lock build.
And don't tell her I helped. She will be insufferable about it.

e04_n04_hale_tip.objective: Hale contributes operationally, but involving him affects Maya.

e04_n05_signal_locked.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [SIGNAL LOCKED]
There.
Give me a second.
I forgot how to talk to someone without checking if they were already dead.
My name is Maya.
Maya Chen.
I was starting to think I'd die talking to static.
I'm pinned beside a damaged relay console in the lower access spine.
The console casing is cracked open. One support strut is bent across my left leg.
I can move it, but not quietly. And not without using oxygen I don't have.
And if Ethan Hale is on your other channel...
Don't let him steer you.

e04_n05_signal_locked.objective: Maya becomes a living person with a physical location and urgent survival objective.

e04_n06_maya_position.speaker: MAYA

events.title: SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN
events.content:
CHANNEL SOURCE: 7.14 MHz
VOICEPRINT MATCH: MAYA CHEN
LIFE SIGNS: ACTIVE
OXYGEN: 18%
LOCATION ESTIMATE: DAMAGED RELAY CONSOLE / LOWER ACCESS SPINE
VISUAL FEED: UNAVAILABLE
STATUS: ALIVE / CRITICAL
events.caption: Automated survivor verification generated from channel telemetry. No image recovered.

events.text: That's me.
Alive.
For now.
Oxygen is at eighteen percent. It feels lower.
The relay console is holding part of the debris off my leg.
I can stay long enough to pull local telemetry, or I can move before my lungs start making decisions for me.
I don't love either option.

e04_n06_maya_position.objective: First true operation decision: data vs survival pace.

choices.text: MOVE MAYA TOWARD THE ACCESS SPINE.

e04_n07_console_route.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Staying with the console.
I named it Malcolm.
The cracked relay console. Don't judge me. You get lonely enough, everything with a blinking light starts having a personality.
Malcolm is not my friend, but Malcolm is keeping debris off my leg, so we're being professional.
I need both hands for this. If I keep talking, I'm going to drop the local buffer.

e04_n07_console_route.objective: Staying gives a reward but reveals danger.

e04_n07_move_route.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Moving now.
Good. I was one bad thought away from arguing with Malcolm again.
I'm going to pull myself clear and head for the access spine.
If I swear, pretend the channel corrupted it.

e04_n07_move_route.objective: Moving preserves oxygen but loses early telemetry.

e04_n08_hale_interrupt.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: [DUAL TRANSMISSION DETECTED]
Don't trust her.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Of course that's the first thing he says.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Maya—
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. Not yet.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Operator, listen to me. Hale is very good at sounding reasonable while the room burns.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That is not fair.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Is it false?
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: He will make this about procedure. About logs. About proof.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And while you're asking the clean questions, something down here keeps moving.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm not asking you to trust me forever.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm asking you to get me through the next door.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n08_hale_interrupt.objective: Player manages interpersonal conflict while Maya is physically vulnerable.

e04_n09_mute_hale.speaker: MAYA

events.text: [HALE CHANNEL TEMPORARILY MUTED]
Thank you.
I know that probably felt wrong.
He makes silence feel like betrayal.
I don't have much air, so I'm going to be blunt.
Hale is dead. I am not saying that because I heard a rumor.
I saw what was left of the corridor.
And now I need to get through Service Lock C before whatever is using his signal notices I'm moving.

e04_n09_mute_hale.objective: Maya opens up more if Hale is muted, but Hale may remember this later.

e04_n09_both_channels.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Fine. Let him listen.
But if he starts steering this conversation, I cut the channel myself.
Technically you would interrupt the carrier.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: See?
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I was being helpful.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You were being you.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Operator, the longer both channels stay open, the uglier the carrier gets.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Then help instead of narrating the problem.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n09_both_channels.objective: Keeping both channels preserves Hale but increases signal stress.

e04_n10_route_choice.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm at a split.
Left is a maintenance crawlway. Tight enough that my shoulders already hate it.
Right is the main access spine. Faster. More open.
There's a reflection in the panel glass on the right side.
It moved before I did.
I hate reflections. They always know things first.

e04_n10_route_choice.objective: Second operation decision: safe/slow vs fast/dangerous.

e04_n11_crawlway.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Crawlway.
Great. I love survival choices that involve becoming luggage.
I'm going in head-first. Radio clipped to my collar.
If the signal drops, it's because I'm dragging myself through a metal throat.

e04_n11_crawlway.objective: Safer path preserves stealth but plants horror.

e04_n11_main_spine.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Main spine.
Fast and stupid. Sometimes that works.
I'm crossing now. Keeping the channel low.

e04_n11_main_spine.objective: Faster path saves oxygen but raises Maya's stress and unknown attention.

e04_n12_crew_memory.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Service Lock C still has pressure.
Barely. It keeps cycling like it can't decide whether to save me or make a point.
There were six of us assigned to ECHO.
Hale. Rourke. Lin. Patel. Arman. Me.
Rourke snored through pressure drills.
Lin labeled everything, including things that were already labels.
Patel pretended he hated everyone and fixed their coffee ratios when they weren't looking.
Arman sang when he was nervous.
He sang a lot near the end.
Records keep death times.
They don't keep people.

e04_n12_crew_memory.objective: Conversation happens during movement, not instead of movement.

choices.text: WHAT DO YOU NEED RIGHT NOW?

e04_n13_proof.speaker: MAYA

events.text: You want proof. Good. Keep wanting proof.
But understand this: the station is very good at handing you records after everyone who could explain them is gone.
You will find logs. Timestamps. Clean little labels for ugly things.
What you won't find is the sound Hale made when the corridor decompressed.
I still hear it anyway.
If you need proof, get me to the service lock terminal.
I can pull the survivor statement from my side.

e04_n13_proof.objective: Proof path increases tension but gives a concrete next action.

e04_n13_need.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Right now?
Air.
After that, water.
After that, a room where nothing knows my name.
Operationally: I need Service Lock C open without cycling the outer seal.
Emotionally: ask me later. Preferably when my lungs stop voting against me.
Get me to the terminal. I can send a statement and maybe keep myself alive.

e04_n13_need.objective: Survival-focused path strengthens Maya as a person and a rescue target.

e04_n14_terminal.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm moving to the terminal.
It's only a few meters. Which is a very long distance when every breath feels rented.

e04_n14_terminal.objective: End on a rescue decision that affects Maya's emotional state going into Episode 05.

e04_r07_console_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r07_console_return.objective: Maya returns after extracting telemetry from the damaged relay console.

events.text: I'm back.
That took longer than it should have. The buffer kept dropping whenever I shifted my weight.
My leg is not broken. I think. It hurts in a way that feels negotiable.

events.title: MAYA RELAY TELEMETRY
events.content:
LOWER ACCESS SPINE
OXYGEN BLEED: ACTIVE
AIRFLOW SOURCE: SERVICE LOCK C
SECONDARY MOVEMENT TRACE: DETECTED NEAR DOOR 12 RETURN LINE
NOTE: trace does not match Maya's current position.
events.caption: Maya extracted this while stabilizing the damaged relay console.

events.text: Movement trace near the Door 12 return line.
That is not me.
So. Good news: we got data.
Bad news: something else is moving.

e04_r07_move_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r07_move_return.objective: Maya returns after leaving the damaged console.

events.text: I'm clear.
Not graceful. Not quiet. But clear.
I'm in the access spine now.
Narrow. Cold. The wall panels are sweating condensation.
It smells like hot plastic and old blood.
I don't have the telemetry from the console.
But I'm farther from the oxygen leak.
That's a trade I can live with. Literally.

e04_r11_crawlway_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r11_crawlway_return.objective: Maya returns after clearing the crawlway.

events.text: I'm through.
Slow. Quiet. Awful.
There are scratch marks inside the paneling.
Not tool marks. Fingernails.
Somebody tried to get through here in the dark.
I'm going to pretend I didn't see that until my body has time to schedule a reaction.

e04_r11_main_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r11_main_return.objective: Maya returns after crossing the main access spine.

events.text: I'm at Service Lock C.
Something moved behind me halfway across.
I ran.
Not gracefully.
I hit my shoulder on a hatch frame and said a word that would get me banned from two mission briefings.
But I'm here. Door is in front of me.

e04_r14_terminal_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r14_terminal_return.objective: Maya returns from the Service Lock C terminal with her survivor statement.

events.text: I'm at the terminal.
Screen is cracked. Buttons are sticky. I am choosing not to identify the substance.

events.title: SURVIVOR STATEMENT: MAYA
events.content:
TEXT TRANSCRIPT / CHANNEL 7.14

MAYA CHEN:
"There were six assigned crew members on ECHO. After the breach, only one confirmed biological survivor remained: me. The signal using Ethan Hale's identity is not a living crew member. Do not allow it to control station routing."

Transcript confidence: 91%
events.caption: Text transcript reconstructed from Maya's stabilized emergency channel.

events.text: One confirmed biological survivor.
That phrase sounds so clean.
Like the station is politely avoiding the word alone.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT THE VICTIM
No.
No, don't listen to that.

upgradeFocus: Maya contact rewritten to feel like a real survivor conversation.
ProgressTask movement/work sections converted to characterBusy with return nodes.
No visible wait timer expected; return handled by engine busyState.
Hale and Maya conflict rewritten as human friction instead of exposition.
Scenario spine preserved: 7.14 lock, Maya identity, lower spine, Hale conflict, route choice, service lock, survivor statement.
Exactly two visible choices preserved for all decision groups.

summary: Progress bars replaced where appropriate with characterBusy + returnNodeId.
Maya speaks like a real exhausted survivor, not a lore dispenser.
Hale/Maya conflict is shown through behavior and friction, not UI trust alerts.
Every visible choice group has exactly two choices.
Physical actions and movement produce return reports.
No visible wait timer is exposed to the player.
Lore is delivered through lived experience, environment, and recovered evidence.
